갈라디아서 3:1.content: Foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly portrayed among you as crucified?

갈라디아서 3:2.content: I just want to learn this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

갈라디아서 3:3.content: Are you so foolish? Having begun in the Spirit, are you now completed in the flesh?

갈라디아서 3:4.content: Did you suffer so many things in vain, if it is indeed in vain?

갈라디아서 3:5.content: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

갈라디아서 3:6.content: Even as Abraham “believed God, and it was counted to him for righteousness.”

갈라디아서 3:7.content: Know therefore that those who are of faith are children of Abraham.

갈라디아서 3:8.content: The Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the Good News beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you all the nations will be blessed.”

갈라디아서 3:9.content: So then, those who are of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham.

갈라디아서 3:10.content: For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who doesn’t continue in all things that are written in the book of the law, to do them.”

갈라디아서 3:11.content: Now that no man is justified by the law before God is evident, for, “The righteous will live by faith.”

갈라디아서 3:12.content: The law is not of faith, but, “The man who does them will live by them.”

갈라디아서 3:13.content: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree,”

갈라디아서 3:14.content: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

갈라디아서 3:15.content: Brothers, speaking of human terms, though it is only a man’s covenant, yet when it has been confirmed, no one makes it void or adds to it.

갈라디아서 3:16.content: Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his offspring. He doesn’t say, “To descendants”, as of many, but as of one, “To your offspring”, which is Christ.

갈라디아서 3:17.content: Now I say this: A covenant confirmed beforehand by God in Christ, the law, which came four hundred thirty years after, does not annul, so as to make the promise of no effect.

갈라디아서 3:18.content: For if the inheritance is of the law, it is no more of promise; but God has granted it to Abraham by promise.

갈라디아서 3:19.content: Then why is there the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring should come to whom the promise has been made. It was ordained through angels by the hand of a mediator.

갈라디아서 3:20.content: Now a mediator is not between one, but God is one.

갈라디아서 3:21.content: Is the law then against the promises of God? Certainly not! For if there had been a law given which could make alive, most certainly righteousness would have been of the law.

갈라디아서 3:22.content: But the Scripture imprisoned all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.

갈라디아서 3:23.content: But before faith came, we were kept in custody under the law, confined for the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

갈라디아서 3:24.content: So that the law has become our tutor to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

갈라디아서 3:25.content: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a tutor.

갈라디아서 3:26.content: For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus.

갈라디아서 3:27.content: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

갈라디아서 3:28.content: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

갈라디아서 3:29.content: If you are Christ’s, then you are Abraham’s offspring and heirs according to promise.
